latest.title: Form 8.3 - Reabold Resources plc
latest.content:
24 Jun 2026 14:44
RNS Number : 6242J
Reabold Resources PLC
24 June 2026
FORM 8.3
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY
A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE
Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Full name of discloser:
James Reid & close relatives
(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):
The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient. For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.
(c) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
Reabold Resources plc
(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeror/offeree, state this and specify identity of offeror/offeree:
(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
23rd June 2026
(f) In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
NO
2. POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
100p ordinary
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
215,000
1.48
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
215,000
1.48
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
100p ordinary
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
393,750 warrants on a 1:1 basis, exercisable at a strike price of 110p; date of grant: 27th April 2026, date of expiry: 31st March 2029
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchase/sale
Number of securities
Price per unit
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO
Date of disclosure:
24th June 2026
Contact name:
James Reid
Telephone number*:
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact

or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. company_30s.what_they_do: Reabold Beacon Limited is a holding company that builds and manages a portfolio of small to medium‑sized businesses in the energy, technology and infrastructure sectors. It acquires companies, injects capital, and then lets the subsidiaries grow under its umbrella.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The group is positioning itself in high‑growth niches such as renewable energy, data‑centre power and digital infrastructure. These areas have long‑term demand from governments and corporates, and the company’s focus on acquiring undervalued assets gives it a potential competitive edge in a crowded market.
company_30s.current_state: The company just started trading after a series of acquisitions and a fresh capital raise. It is still in a cash‑burn phase, building its portfolio and waiting for the first revenue streams to materialise. The stock is trading well below the valuation implied by its assets, reflecting the early‑stage nature of the business.

rns_narrative: Reabold has been busy turning its capital‑raising momentum into tangible assets – the Beacon acquisition, a $16 m earn‑out for Colle Santo, and new investment into LNEnergy all point to a growing portfolio.

Corporate housekeeping is underway: a recent AGM confirmed the board’s strategy, a deferred bonus plan was announced, and a group of strategic investors has added fresh cash. These moves suggest the management is keeping the company afloat while laying the groundwork for growth.

Regulatory progress is a clear theme – a new licence for the Danube Parta project, a formal decree, and a milestone in West Newton’s approval pipeline indicate the group is clearing the legal hurdles that often slow energy and tech deals.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious optimism amid a flurry of regulatory and funding updates
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the company’s new investor hub, fresh funding, and the acquisition of promising energy assets as signs that the group is finally gaining traction and could unlock value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of current revenue, the long regulatory timelines, and the fact that the company is still a shell that has to prove it can generate cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is slowly shifting from neutral to mildly bullish as recent announcements start to paint a picture of a company that is actively moving forward rather than idling.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, which means the market is currently unaware of any catalyst – a perfect setting for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Approval of the Danube Parta licence – this unlocks a sizeable renewable project that could drive early revenue.
Completion of the Colle Santo earn‑out – the agreed payment would add cash to the balance sheet.
Quarterly earnings release – a positive surprise on revenue or cash flow would validate the strategy.
A surge in trading volume accompanying any of the above news would confirm market interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include: a breakout above the recent 30‑day low with volume at least 1.5× average, the announcement of a new license or funding round, and any mention of a first revenue‑generating project in the next 60 days.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share has likely already hit its bottom – the depth of the recent crash suggests early capitulation. The APEX score indicates a tight squeeze, so a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once a catalyst clears the regulatory hurdle.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits far below the value implied by its growing asset base. If the company secures the Danube licence and starts cash flow, the price could climb to the midpoint of the 52‑week range – roughly a 200‑250 % upside from today. The RED‑style crash gives the biggest reward potential in the historical data, so a 1‑3 risk‑reward is realistic.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that regulatory approvals stall or the company cannot turn its acquisitions into cash generators. If the project timeline extends beyond 12 months, the price could stay near the current low and the upside would evaporate.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100 % of the initial stake if the company remains a shell. Potential gain: 200 % if the price rebounds to the mid‑52‑week high. Risk‑reward ratio roughly 1:2.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume‑backed breakout above the 30‑day low, set a stop 5 % below that low to protect against a false break. Allocate no more than 5 % of your portfolio to keep the position small relative to the upside potential.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a key catalyst – licence approval or earnings surprise – is announced. Watch for a 20 % jump in volume coupled with a price lift of at least 10 % to confirm momentum.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal risk is prolonged regulatory delay.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, clear regulatory catalysts, and a highly asymmetric upside make Reabold Beacon a contrarian bullish play.
